title: sida schumanniana
intro: Sida schumanniana, also known as the Schumann's sida or hairy sida, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sida schumanniana include its potential to treat various health conditions such as fever, wounds, and skin infections, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and astringent. Sida schumanniana is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
